meta.name: Arjun Pareek
meta.thesis: I architect agentic AI systems — multi-agent pipelines with human-in-the-loop control.

projects.title: RAG Interview Assistant
projects.tagline: Retrieval-augmented interview prep grounded in your own resume
highlights: ~500-char chunking strategy — sharper vectors than full-document embeddings
Local embedding model chosen for zero training-data privacy exposure
projects.story: First step of a deliberate capability ladder: retrieval before orchestration. Markdown case study here — what it does, one architecture decision, one thing learned.

projects.title: Multi-Agent Interview Prep
projects.tagline: Tool-using agents built on raw Anthropic tool use — no framework
highlights: Orchestration loop hand-built: model signals tool_use and stops; my layer executes and returns results
Full message-history management across every API call
projects.story: Built without LangChain on purpose — to understand the stop_reason mechanics frameworks hide.

projects.title: CareerBot
projects.tagline: Multi-agent job pipeline with human-in-the-loop approval gates
highlights: Three cooperating agents: resume management, ATS job discovery (Greenhouse/Lever/Ashby), per-job tailoring
interrupt/Command(resume) approval gates; two-node split so expensive work never re-fires on resume
SQLite-backed checkpointing; centralized state restoration layer
projects.story: The system that applies architecture lessons from RAG and tool use before it. Human approval is a first-class graph node, not an afterthought.

projects.title: Damage Claim Verifier
highlights: Two-stage perception/decision architecture — model observes, Python decides
Schema-enforced structured output via Pydantic tool calls
Truth hierarchy enforced architecturally: history is not a parameter to the verdict function
A/B-tested prompting; single-pass beat reasoning-first on both accuracy and speed
projects.story: HackerRank Orchestrate, June 2026. Highest Judge Interview score in the top 8.

projects.title: RAG Support Triage Agent
highlights: Escalation routing: similarity threshold + LLM self-assessment signal
projects.story: HackerRank Orchestrate, May 2026. The run that set up June's top-1% finish — include the delta between the two as the narrative.
